Title: A Toxin of Valsa mali Determines Virulence and Host Preference

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: p-coumaric acid ethyl ester (p-CAEE) toxin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Backbone-forming core step; upstream/committed step for p-CAEE production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required for biosynthesis of the toxin p-coumaric acid ethyl ester; exact chemical conversion not resolved in the paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms or is required for formation of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for p-CAEE production and virulence-associated host preference
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxic compound that enhances fungal infection and virulence on apple
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In fungal culture filtrate/secondary metabolite extracts during infection-related growth
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Malus × domestica; Pyrus spp.; Valsa pyri
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: RP27 promoter (constitutive, for heterologous overexpression in V. pyri)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly upregulated during infection of apple tissues at 12 and 72 h post-inoculation relative to 0 h
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection stage; exogenous p-CAEE application; heterologous expression in V. pyri; dsRNA targeting VmPKS5
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing/SMBGC annotation referenced from prior study; HPLC metabolite profiling in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; complementation; heterologous overexpression in V. pyri; HPLC metabolite profiling; toxin purification and structural identification by ESI-MS and NMR; exogenous p-CAEE rescue assay; virulence and biomass assays; dsRNA-mediated gene silencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes p-CAEE production and increases virulence on apple; deletion abolishes p-CAEE and strongly reduces virulence on apple but not pear; heterologous expression increases V. pyri virulence on apple
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant effect on vegetative growth reported upon deletion or overexpression; host effect is apple-biased rather than pear
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for spray-induced gene silencing and selective disease control of apple Valsa canker; can be targeted to suppress toxin production and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as determinant of host preference between apple and pear. V. pyri lacks a VmPKS5 homologue, and introducing VmPKS5 confers p-CAEE production and stronger apple virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmPKS5 was one of four V. mali-specific SMBGC core genes induced during infection, and the only tested one with a strong virulence phenotype. 2. Deletion of VmPKS5 abolished p-CAEE production and greatly reduced virulence and fungal biomass on apple, but not on pear. 3. Exogenous p-CAEE partly rescued the ΔVmPKS5 mutant and enhanced wild-type virulence. 4. Heterologous expression of VmPKS5 in V. pyri enabled p-CAEE production and increased virulence toward apple. 5. Exogenous VmPKS5-dsRNA reduced VmPKS5 expression, fungal biomass, and disease symptoms.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Secondary metabolite biosynthetic gene cluster associated with VmPKS5
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Cluster-associated enzyme; specific step not established
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Contains PKS-related domains and is infection-induced, but its precise catalytic role was not functionally validated in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Significantly upregulated during pathogen infection stage
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although infection-induced and located in the same cluster as VmPKS5, VmPKS6 was not directly functionally tested for metabolite or virulence effects in the Results and is not a primary experimentally resolved gene-metabolite determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmPKS6 is the second secondary metabolism-related gene in the PKS5 cluster. 2. Its transcript abundance increases during infection. 3. SMART analysis supports a PKS-domain architecture. 4. Its direct role in p-CAEE biosynthesis was not established in this paper.
